note: Strong bluish cast. Seed 0 reads '5 CENT', seed 2 reads '1 CENT'. Seed 3 is bimetallic with big '1': gold ring, silver center (1euro). Seed 1 numeral unreadable (noise wall) but dark copper tone and measured radius 79.8 vs 5cent 95.9 gives ~18.7mm -> 2cent. Mild perspective: scale shrinks toward top of frame.
Seed 0 reads '5 CENT', seed 2 reads '1 CENT' with globe. Seed 1 unreadable but dark copper, radius 86.5 vs 5cent 98.3 at same height -> ~18.7mm = 2cent. Same coin trio as image_121 minus the 1euro.

Single very dark coin on smooth pink background; multiple denoise/contrast attempts recover no relief or numeral (pure noise). No second coin for relative scale and color is crushed by heavy blue cast (coin BGR ~83/39/30). Truly unreadable; dark patina hints copper family but denomination indeterminable.

Top coin clearly reads '10 CENT' but has NO green circle -> added as extra (cx 414, cy 378, r 68). Seed 0 shows a faint slanted '1' with globe; seed0/seed1 radius ratio 67.5/83.8=0.805 best matches 1cent/5cent (0.765). Seed 1 dark worn copper, national side, largest copper -> 5cent. Both bottom labels size+glimpse based, not fully readable.

Seed 0 reads '20' (upside down); coin looks silvery due to color degradation but numeral is decisive. Upper-left coin has NO green circle -> extra 1euro: bimetallic with '1' over bars, gold ring, silver center (cx 244, cy 396, r 78). Radii ratio 77.7/74.8 matches 1euro/20cent diameters.

Seed 2 clearly reads '10' (greenish-gold cast) -> 10cent, used as scale anchor (r=76). Seed 1 shows faint '1' with globe, r=63.5 -> ~16.5mm = 1cent. Seed 0 shows worn national side, dark copper, r=71.3 -> ~18.5mm = 2cent (5cent would need r>81).
